tickets.title: Name the Lens: the square drawn in the glossary, taught in the tutorial (divergence preserved as a follow-on)
tickets.body:
BUILD-READY, DOCS + TUTORIAL, no src/ behavior change. Decided with Myk in chat (2026-07-17): the
word "lens" — the spec's most load-bearing piece of prose — gets NAMED, DRAWN, and DEMONSTRATED so
nobody is ever confused about it again. And the lens-divergence idea is preserved here as a taught
concept and a named follow-on, deliberately NOT built as a tool (no consumer exists yet — see the
follow-on clause).

THE CONCEPT TO NAME (settled in chat, restate it exactly): a LENS is not a type — it is the prose
name for the whole reading-side assembly. The four exported types form a square with two altitudes:

    living:   HyperSchema --x ground--> HyperView --x Schema--> View
                 | reifies                             | reifies
    frozen:   VersionedHyperSchema (FUTURE)         VersionedSchema (name@hash)

- HyperSchema: the gather program (which deltas, bucketed how). HyperView: the gathered evidence,
  all voices present. Schema: the resolution program ({props: Map<field, Policy>, default}) — which
  claims win, what fields mean. View: the resolved answer.
- The generating arrows each take a second input: HyperSchema x ground -> HyperView; Schema x
  HyperView -> View. Nothing generates from a definition alone.
- A LENS is one choice of rung per column PLUS the binding's stance (roots, writable; resolvers fold
  into the versioned rung per SS22.4). The LIVING lens evolves and is what the latest door serves; a
  PINNED lens reads through frozen rungs forever (SS17/SS23). SS26's asOf pins the ground itself —
  orthogonal to both.
- The square is currently ASYMMETRIC on purpose: VersionedSchema is built; VersionedHyperSchema is
  the named-deferred rung (spec/21 — "build it when a pin needs the gather frozen"). Draw it as
  pencil, say so.
- Forking at ANY rung yields a different lens (T2's coexistence is the rung-3 case: two Schemas over
  one gather). Lens identity is a tuple of content addresses, one per rung — the system already
  mints every one of them.

DO:
1. spec/18-glossary.md gains the LENS entry — the square drawn (ASCII, as above), the two altitudes,
   the five rungs, the it-is-prose-not-a-type rule and WHY (vocabulary must not fork into
   near-synonyms; CLAUDE.md's standing rule cites this). Cross-reference SS4, SS17, SS21, SS22, SS26.
2. spec/21-schema-identity.md gains a short "the lens, drawn" passage near the ladder (edit-in-place,
   the rare correction case): the same square, one paragraph, pointing at the glossary for the full
   treatment. Do NOT restate the ladder — link the two pictures.
3. THE TUTORIAL ACT (demos/tutorial — SS19's needs-before-doctrine voice): a lesson where the reader
   builds one gather, wears TWO lenses over it (the village's Townbook/FirstImpressions shape,
   miniaturized), pins one (name@hash), evolves the other, and SEES the square come apart and hold:
   the pinned reading never moves, the living one does, the ground is shared. Close the lesson with
   the DIVERGENCE MOMENT: print the two lenses' rung-hashes side by side (termHash, lineage,
   snapshot hash, roots) and let the reader see "these agree down to rung 3" with their own eyes —
   membership of the concept without an API. Follow demos/tutorial/lessons.mjs's existing lesson
   shape; run the tutorial to verify the lesson actually executes.
4. demos/village/README.md ledger entry if (and only if) the village is touched — the tutorial is
   the demonstration surface here; the village already demonstrates coexistence itself.

THE NAMED FOLLOW-ON (preserve verbatim, do not build): LENS DIVERGENCE as a first-class surface — a
comparison of two lenses reporting the deepest differing rung (1 gather body / 2 gather pin [waits
for VersionedHyperSchema] / 3 resolution lineage / 4 resolution pin / 5 binding stance), computed
purely from already-minted content addresses. Decided in chat (Myk, 2026-07-17): it graduates to its
own ticket WHEN a consumer arrives — the promote-LAW review (SS24.4) and federation-drift tooling are
the anticipated callers; a divergence PROFILE (which rungs differ), not a scalar metric, is the
honest shape. Until then the tutorial's printed comparison IS the feature.

DO NOT: mint a Lens type or a lensDistance API; renumber sections; restate the SS21 ladder in the
glossary (link it); let the tutorial lesson exceed the existing lessons' length register.

RAILS: the tutorial's own harness (the arc test over lessons) must stay green with the new lesson
executing; prettier/lint clean; no src/ diff at all (the ticket's boundary — a src change found
necessary is NEWS, stop and surface it).

MERGE: docs + tutorial only, no behavior, no migration -> self-mergeable once green and P5-clean.

tickets.title: Adopt rhizomatic 0.8 — `expand` names the child's reading (registry + migration + every expand body)
tickets.body:
rhizomatic 0.8.0 landed the substrate ask from #23 (Loam's own filing): an `expand` term now names BOTH halves of the child's lens — `schema` (how the child gathers) and the new required `reading` (the resolution Schema the child resolves through). Resolving an expansion under a legacy body (no `reading`) is a LOUD ERROR — there is no fallback to the parent's Schema (which was the pre-0.8 behavior, correct only in narrow edge cases). This ticket is the compatibility floor: adopt 0.8 so Loam does not crash, and migrate every store that holds an expand body. The FEATURE payoff (resolvers actually reaching expanded children — attributed timeline posts) is a SEPARATE ticket, T26, that depends on this one.

The jump is 0.6.0 → 0.8.0, so it also pulls in 0.7.0's strict Ed25519 acceptance criterion (#20): transparent for every honest signature Loam produces, but it is a federation-relevant verification change — name it in the PR for Myk's merge.

SCOPE (blast radius mapped 2026-07-18):
1. Version bump `@bombadil/rhizomatic` ^0.6.0 → ^0.8.0 in package.json; regenerate lockfile.
2. Thread `readings` into ALL SchemaRegistry.build call sites — 6 in src (lifecycle.ts:245, :286, :352, :401, :509; gateway.ts:202 empty default) + test/spike/schema.test.ts:102. Source the readings map (reading-name → Schema) from the bound Schemas already on each Program/Bound, keyed by lensName — add a `programReadings` helper beside `programHyperschemas` (lifecycle.ts:236).
3. Import the new 0.8 symbols where used (absent everywhere today): `schemaHash`, `resolveReading`, `getReading`, `collectReadingRefs` — chiefly in lifecycle.ts, registration.ts, migrate.ts. CONFIRM against the installed 0.8 dist whether `resolveView` (reads.ts:94/137/184) needs a registry/readings arg to resolve a nested child, or whether the self-describing hview carries the reading content address and resolveView stays 2-arg (the changelog says the resolve API surface is untouched and serialized hviews carry the reading's content address — verify).
4. Add `reading` to EVERY expand body: demos/pachyderm/bundle/{feed,latest}.json (reading "Post"), demos/larder/bundle/grocery-list.json (the child Schema of role item — confirm its Schema NAME from item.json), demos/planner/bundle/planner-book.json (the Plan reading), demos/village/schemas/{circle,film-night}.json + the generator demos/village/gen-schemas.mjs (expandThrough must emit reading), scripts/gen-packets.mjs, demos/tutorial/lessons.mjs (FILM_EXPAND_BODY), and test fixtures test/spike/schema.test.ts, test/gateway/{genesis,read,link}.test.ts (the `expand role plant schema Plant` bodies gain reading "Plant").
5. §20 MIGRATION step (this is a breaking on-wire change → mandatory, same PR): a 4th entry in MIGRATIONS (src/migrate/migrate.ts). It shape-detects a hyperschema DEFINITION delta whose body contains an `expand` lacking `reading`, and re-signs the definition with `reading` filled from the child Schema name — MECHANICAL because pre-0.8 stores are single-lens-per-hyperschema (the changelog's own migration checklist: exactly one candidate reading per hyperschema, since coexistence postdates them). It must be idempotent: the discriminator is 'expand present AND reading absent' (distinct by construction once reading is filled), mirroring the naive-shape caveat comment at migrate.ts:57. Walk pinned `{pinned:<hash>}` refs too if Loam pins any body hashes (it pins VersionedSchema snapshots by hash, but those are resolution Schemas, not hyperschema bodies — CONFIRM no body-hash pin exists that would break).
6. spec/22 LANDS the resolved behavior (this realizes the old T25 spec-doc half): the boundary note is REPLACED by the landed truth — an expand names the child's reading, the child resolves through its own Schema. The Provenance footer cites this PR and rhizomatic#23 / 0.8.0.
7. Village: fresh-run the village end-to-end; the reading-bearing schemas must certify green. Update demos/village/README.md's ledger.

RAILS to freeze (P3, write first): a store carrying a pre-0.8 expand body, run through migrate, resolves its expansions without the loud error (the migration filled reading); a village/demo expand read still returns the child view; the SchemaRegistry.build readings arg is exercised (an unknown reading ref fails registry build LOUDLY at publish, caught quietly on replay). MERGE: Myk's — breaking on-wire change + migration + federation-relevant (0.7 strict sig).

tickets.title: Resolvers reach expanded children — attributed timeline posts, closing pachy.2
tickets.body:
With rhizomatic 0.8 adopted (T25), an `expand` names the child's reading, so rhizomatic resolves an expanded child through the child's own Policies. But Loam's §22 host-level RESOLVERS (the attribution byline, the RSVP provenance) are applied by applyResolvers (src/gateway/resolvers.ts:177) only to a lens's OWN top-level fields — it explicitly does NOT descend into expanded children (see the renderTarget comment at resolvers.ts:49: 'Expansions … an edge story rung (a) does not reach into'). This ticket makes resolvers reach expanded children: for each field that is an expansion, look up the child reading's ResolverSpecs (Loam holds these per-lens, keyed by lensName in registration.ts) and recurse applyResolvers into the child view with the child hview and child root. The reading name for each expanded field is now knowable — it is on the expand term (static in the body) and on the self-describing hview entry's reading content address.

PAYOFF: this closes the gap Pachyderm documented honestly at pachyderm.mjs pachy.2 — the attribution resolver (post.json's `text`, which signs the byline from cryptographic authorship) applies on a DIRECT post read but not to posts as expanded children of the feed. After this ticket, the timeline itself carries attributed posts. Update pachy.2's comment from documenting the gap to asserting the fix, and strengthen the check to require attribution in the EXPANDED (timeline) read, not only the direct read.

RAILS (P3, write first): a direct read of an entity and the SAME entity read as an expanded child of a feed produce the SAME resolver-decorated value (attribution present in both) — the test that would have caught the original gap; the memo still invalidates on erasure for a child-applied resolver (the child bucket's delta-set keys the memo, same as top-level); a lens whose child reading has NO resolvers is unchanged (the raw child view passes through). No new wire bytes — this is a pure read-path enrichment, so no migration. MERGE: rides T25's PR or stacks on it; Myk's merge because it lands atop the breaking change and touches the read path across every door.
